figures.caption: Figure 1: Computational domains and meshes employed in the simulations. Only the elements (without the Gauss-Lobatto-Legendre points) are shown in the NEK5000 grid for enhanced visibility. In the ELLIPSYS grid, every third cell in each direction is exhibited.
Figure 2: Wall grid resolution in the spanwise (𝑧), wall-normal (𝑦), and streamwise (𝑥) directions on the suction side (SS, solid lines) and pressure side (PS, dashed lines).
Figure 3: Evolution of streamwise velocity fluctuations (maximum over the normal direction) for narrow (𝐿𝑧 = 0.1) and wide (𝐿𝑧 = 0.2) domains.
Figure 4: Instantaneous isosurfaces on the suction side of the airfoil for narrow and wide domains at 𝑇 = 2.0𝑐/𝑈∞.
Figure 5: Isosurfaces of time-averaged streamwise velocity for several levels of reverse flow.
Figure 6: Comparison of pressure and wall-friction coefficients.
Figure 8: Comparison of the spanwise- and time-averaged horizontal (𝑈) and vertical (𝑉) velocities in the near field of the airfoil.
Figure 9: Comparison of the normal profiles of streamwise and normal velocities on the suction side. A scale of 0.1 corresponds to a velocity of 1.4 in the plot of 𝑈 and 0.2 in the one of 𝑉 . .
Figure 10: Comparison of the normal profiles of streamwise and normal velocities on the pressure side. A scale of 0.1 corresponds to a velocity of 1.4 in the plot of 𝑈 and 0.2 in the one of 𝑉 .

Figure 11: Amplitude spectra of velocity perturbations on the suction side at several streamwise stations. and indicate streamwise and normal velocity perturbations, respectively, obtained with NEK5000. and are the corresponding perturbations obtained with ELLIPSYS.

Figure 12: Amplitude spectra of velocity perturbations on the pressure side at several streamwise stations. and indicate streamwise and normal velocity perturbations, respectively, obtained with NEK5000. and are the corresponding perturbations obtained with ELLIPSYS.
Figure 13: Contours of 𝑁 factor from PSE. Isolines of the real part of the streamwise wavenumber are show in black. Locus of maximum amplification is highlighted with a red line.

Figure 14: Comparison between PSE and LES computations from NEK5000 and ELLIPSYS for the mode with the highest 𝑁 factor ( 𝑓 = 15, 𝛽 = 0) on the suction side. The vertical line in panel (b) indicates the streamwise location where the perturbation reaches O(10−2).

Figure 15: Real part of the first SPOD mode for streamwise and normal velocity perturbations for 𝑓 = 15, 𝛽 = 0. The amplitude of the mode corresponds to 𝜆1, i.e., the eigenvalue relative to the first mode. The gray line indicates the displacement thickness (𝛿∗), and the green line denotes the LSB edge (𝑦𝑏).
Figure 16: Evolution of the normal force coefficient. SIN is the function 𝐶𝑁 (𝑡) = 0.4583 + 0.04795 sin (0.1309𝑡 − 0.6055).
Figure 17: Compilation of 𝑆𝑡 = ( 𝑓 ∗𝑐/𝑈∞) sin 𝐴𝑜𝐴 from the literature and current work (Fava (2023), FFA-W3).
